title: Cannot delete Docker images from ghcr.io
body:
```sh
skopeo delete docker://ghcr.io/organization/my-application@sha256:DOCKER-HASH-SHA256-IMAGE

FATA[0001] Failed to delete /v2/organization/my-application/manifests/sha256:DOCKER-HASH-SHA256-IMAGE:
{"errors":[{"code":"UNSUPPORTED","message":"The operation is unsupported."}]}
 (405 Method Not Allowed) 
```

I'd like to remove some old images from GitHub Container Registry.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

The client can’t very well substitute for a server just not providing a feature. (https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/233 suggests that this was actually possible, _in principle_ some time ago.)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Please reopen if there’s more to this.

I still have the same issue:

```console
$ skopeo delete docker://ghcr.io/felipecrs/alpine
FATA[0000] Failed to delete /v2/felipecrs/alpine/manifests/sha256:c0669ef34cdc14332c0f1ab0c2c01acb91d96014b172f1a76f3a39e63d1f0bda: {"errors":[{"code":"UNSUPPORTED","message":"The operation is unsupported."}]}
 (405 Method Not Allowed)
```

> The client can’t very well substitute for a server just not providing a feature. (https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/233 suggests that this was actually possible, in principle some time ago.)

The issue you referred to mentioned GCR, not ghcr.io.

I understand that this is server related feature. However, GitHub has allegedly implemented it:

https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/24974#discussioncomment-3246040

But it still doesn't work.

> However, GitHub has allegedly implemented it:

By my very brief attempt to follow the links, that’s some GitHub Packages REST API, not the registry API.
That would make sense. I'll try to ping them for implementing the feature in the registry API side.
